content.problemStatement:
Read problems statements in <a target="_blank"

There is a castle which can be described as an undirected tree with NNN vertices and N−1N - 1N−1 edges. Each edge has a magic number CCC.

When a group of tourists visit the castle, they are interested in a path between vertices UUU and VVV. They think that an edge is **interesting** if its magic number is ≤K\leq K≤K. The total attractiveness of the path is the xor of all interesting edges on it.

Given MMM groups of tourists, find the total attractiveness of the path for each group.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. Then T test case descriptions follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, denoting the number of vertices.
The next N−1N - 1N−1 lines each contain three integers UUU, VVV and CCC, which denote an edge between vertices UUU and VVV with magical number CCC.
The next line contains a single integer MMM, which denotes the number of requests.
The next MMM lines contain information about the requests. Each line contains three integers UUU, VVV and KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each request output a single integer in a separate line - the total attractiveness of the path.
